factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, particularly in the trench, power, and pump segment.

evidence.text: The volume of OEC on rent increased 30.6 percent in our trench, power and pump segment, primarily due to continued strength in our Trench Safety and Power and HVAC regions, and improved performance in our Pump Solutions region.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The summary indicates accelerating demand in key segments, supported by a significant 30.6% increase in volume.

factors.summary: The company experienced a rental rate decrease, indicating pricing power may be limited.

evidence.text: For the nine months ended September 30, 2017 , equipment rental revenue increased 11.7 percent as compared to the same period in 2016 , primarily reflecting a 14.5 percent increase in the volume of OEC on rent, which includes the impact of the NES acquisition discussed in note 2 to the condensed consolidated financial statements, partially offset by a 0.7 percent rental rate decrease.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The evidence shows a rental rate decrease, indicating limited pricing power.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting toward higher-margin segments, particularly in trench, power, and pump services.

evidence.text: The improvement in the Pump Solutions region primarily reflected growth in revenue from i) upstream oil and gas customers, which have experienced significant volatility in recent years, and ii) construction and mining customers.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue is shifting towards higher-margin segments, supported by growth in specific regions.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, driven by increased demand in core markets.

evidence.text: The volume of OEC on rent increased 30.6 percent in our trench, power and pump segment, primarily due to continued strength in our Trench Safety and Power and HVAC regions, and improved performance in our Pump Solutions region.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing significantly, driven by increased demand in core markets.

factors.summary: The trench, power, and pump segment is growing fastest, driven by construction and industrial demand.

evidence.text: The volume of OEC on rent increased 30.6 percent in our trench, power and pump segment, primarily due to continued strength in our Trench Safety and Power and HVAC regions, and improved performance in our Pump Solutions region.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The trench, power, and pump segment is growing fastest, driven by construction and industrial demand.

factors.summary: Margins are expanding, as evidenced by increased EBITDA and adjusted EBITDA.

evidence.text: For the nine months ended September 30, 2017 , EBITDA increased $100 , or 5.1 percent, and adjusted EBITDA increased $207 , or 10.3 percent.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Margins are expanding, as evidenced by significant increases in EBITDA and adjusted EBITDA.

factors.summary: Cost reduction initiatives, such as Lean management techniques, are delivering measurable savings.

evidence.text: We achieved the anticipated run rate savings from the Lean initiatives in 2016 and expect to continue to generate savings from these initiatives.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction initiatives are delivering measurable savings, indicating effective cost management.

factors.summary: Operational efficiency is improving through Lean management techniques.

evidence.text: The implementation of “Lean” management techniques, including kaizen processes focused on continuous improvement . We have trained over 3,100 employees, over 70 percent of our district managers and approximately 55 percent of our branch managers on the Lean kaizen process. We continue to implement this program across our branch network, with the objectives of: reducing the cycle time associated with renting our equipment to customers; improving invoice accuracy and service quality; reducing the elapsed time for equipment pickup and delivery; and improving the effectiveness and efficiency of our repair and maintenance operations.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Operational efficiency is improving through Lean management techniques, supported by specific goals and training.

factors.summary: Management appears confident, as evidenced by their focus on strategic acquisitions and investments in growth.

evidence.text: For the nine months ended September 30, 2017 , equipment rental revenue increased 11.7 percent as compared to the same period in 2016 , primarily reflecting a 14.5 percent increase in the volume of OEC on rent, which includes the impact of the NES acquisition discussed in note 2 to the condensed consolidated financial statements, partially offset by a 0.7 percent rental rate decrease.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management's confidence is evident through strategic acquisitions and revenue growth.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on expanding core equipment rental business and strategic acquisitions.

evidence.text: For the past several years, we have executed a strategy focused on improving the profitability of our core equipment rental business through revenue growth, margin expansion and operational efficiencies. In particular, we have focused on customer segmentation, customer service differentiation, rate management, fleet management and operational efficiency.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Clear, long-term strategic focus on expansion and acquisitions indicates strong positive direction.

factors.summary: Lean management initiatives and strategic acquisitions are key catalysts for growth.

evidence.text: The implementation of “Lean” management techniques, including kaizen processes focused on continuous improvement . We have trained over 3,100 employees, over 70 percent of our district managers and approximately 55 percent of our branch managers on the Lean kaizen process. We continue to implement this program across our branch network, with the objectives of: reducing the cycle time associated with renting our equipment to customers; improving invoice accuracy and service quality; reducing the elapsed time for equipment pickup and delivery; and improving the effectiveness and efficiency of our repair and maintenance operations. We achieved the anticipated run rate savings from the Lean initiatives in 2016 and expect to continue to generate savings from these initiatives.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Successful Lean initiatives and expected savings demonstrate significant positive developments.

factors.summary: The company's competitive advantages include scale, national branch network, and ability to provide a broad range of equipment and services.

evidence.text: We are the largest equipment rental company in the world, with an integrated network of 950 rental locations in the United States and Canada. Although the equipment rental industry is highly fragmented and diverse, we believe that we are well positioned to take advantage of this environment because, as a larger company, we have more extensive resources and certain competitive advantages. These include a fleet of rental equipment with a total original equipment cost (“OEC”) of $10.8 billion , and a national branch network that operates in 49 U.S. states and every Canadian province, and serves 99 of the largest 100 metropolitan areas in the United States.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong competitive advantages in scale and network provide clear positive differentiation.

factors.summary: The company is increasing debt leverage through new senior note issuances.

evidence.text: Since January 1, 2016, we have taken the following actions to improve our financial flexibility and liquidity, and to position us to invest the necessary capital in our business: •Issued $750 principal amount of 4  5 / 8  percent Senior Notes due 2025; •Issued $1.0 billion principal amount of 5  7 / 8  percent Senior Notes due 2026; •Issued $1.0 billion principal amount of 5  1 / 2  percent Senior Notes due 2027; •Issued $1.675 billion principal amount of 4  7 / 8  percent Senior Notes due 2028, comprised of separate issuances of $925 in August 2017 and $750 in September 2017, as discussed in note 8 to the condensed consolidated financial statements;

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Increasing debt leverage introduces financial risk, a moderate headwind.

factors.summary: Strategic acquisitions, such as NES and Neff, are creating value by expanding capabilities.

evidence.text: The pursuit of strategic acquisitions to continue to expand our core equipment rental business , as exhibited by our recently completed acquisitions of NES and Neff. Strategic acquisitions allow us to invest our capital to expand our business, further driving our ability to accomplish our strategic goals.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strategic acquisitions are creating value and expanding capabilities.
